title: [LoopFusion] Optimize away Phi nodes that are sunk from the 2nd loop preheader
author: CongzheUalberta
description:
Fixed #165087.

The bug is that, for the IR from #165087 that caused the crash, after loop fusion the phi instruction in the preheader of the 2nd loop, i.e., `%.sroa.speculated.1.lcssa`, is sunk to the loop exit block, causing the exit block to look like the following:

```
for.cond.cleanup6.2.loopexit:                     ; preds = %for.body11.1
  %.sroa.speculated.2.lcssa = phi i8 [ %.sroa.speculated.1.lcssa, %for.body11.1 ]
  %.sroa.speculated.1.lcssa = phi i8 [ 0, %for.body11.1 ]
  ret i8 %.sroa.speculated.2.lcssa
```

And it caused the assertion "Instruction does not dominate all uses". It does not matter whether `%.sroa.speculated.1.lcssa` was placed before or after `%.sroa.speculated.2.lcssa` in that basic block, the asssertion would always occur since the verifier checked that `%.sroa.speculated.1.lcssa` was not from `%for.body11.1`.

The solution in this MR is that, when we sink phis from the 2nd loop preheader to the exit block, we optimize it a bit further, i.e., propagate the uses of each phi node with its incoming value and optimize away the phis. Those phis only have one incoming value, which is assured by the dominance relationship and `isStrictlyAdjacent()` check as part of the loop fusion legality checks. Deleted `fixPHINodes()` too because the phis are already optimized away and there is no point processing `fixPHINodes()`.

Note that the IR in issue #165087 is not a canonicalized IR and is not something we would usually see before loop fusion in a normal O3 pipeline. In an optimization pipeline InstCombine would optimize away the Phi instructions like `%.sroa.speculated.1.lcssa`. Therefore what is done in this patch aligns with what would happen in an optimization pipeline in practice. It also simplifies current test cases, i.e., `lcssa.ll` and `sunk-phi-nodes.ll`.

tests.test_body:
define void @test1() {
entry:
  br label %vector.ph

vector.ph:                                        ; preds = %middle.block, %entry
  br label %middle.block

middle.block:                                     ; preds = %vector.ph
  br i1 true, label %for.cond17.preheader, label %vector.ph

for.cond17.preheader:                             ; preds = %middle.block
  %.lcssa92 = phi i16 [ 1, %middle.block ]
  br label %vector.ph61

vector.ph61:                                      ; preds = %vector.ph61, %for.cond17.preheader
  br i1 true, label %for.end41, label %vector.ph61

for.end41:                                        ; preds = %vector.ph61
  ret void
}

define i8 @_Z3fn1ssb() {
entry:
  br label %for.body11.1

for.body11.1:                                     ; preds = %for.body11.1, %entry
  %indvars.iv.1 = phi i64 [ %indvars.iv.next.1, %for.body11.1 ], [ 2, %entry ]
  %indvars.iv.next.1 = add i64 %indvars.iv.1, 1
  %exitcond.1.not = icmp eq i64 %indvars.iv.next.1, 0
  br i1 %exitcond.1.not, label %for.body11.2.preheader, label %for.body11.1

for.body11.2.preheader:                           ; preds = %for.body11.1
  %.sroa.speculated.1.lcssa = phi i8 [ 0, %for.body11.1 ]
  br label %for.body11.2

for.body11.2:                                     ; preds = %for.body11.2, %for.body11.2.preheader
  %indvars.iv.2 = phi i64 [ %indvars.iv.next.2, %for.body11.2 ], [ 2, %for.body11.2.preheader ]
  %indvars.iv.next.2 = add i64 %indvars.iv.2, 1
  %exitcond.2.not = icmp eq i64 %indvars.iv.next.2, 0
  br i1 %exitcond.2.not, label %for.cond.cleanup6.2.loopexit, label %for.body11.2

for.cond.cleanup6.2.loopexit:                     ; preds = %for.body11.2
  %.sroa.speculated.2.lcssa = phi i8 [ %.sroa.speculated.1.lcssa, %for.body11.2 ]
  ret i8 %.sroa.speculated.2.lcssa
}

define dso_local i32 @check_sunk_phi_nodes() {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %sum1.02 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.inc ]
  %i.01 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.inc ]
  %add = add nsw i32 %sum1.02, %i.01
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  %inc = add nsw i32 %i.01, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %inc, 10
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.end

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.inc
  %sum1.0.lcssa = phi i32 [ %add, %for.inc ]
  br label %for.body4

for.body4:                                        ; preds = %for.inc6, %for.end
  %i1.04 = phi i32 [ 0, %for.end ], [ %inc7, %for.inc6 ]
  %sum2.03 = phi i32 [ 0, %for.end ], [ %add5, %for.inc6 ]
  %mul = mul nsw i32 %i1.04, %i1.04
  %add5 = add nsw i32 %sum2.03, %mul
  br label %for.inc6

for.inc6:                                         ; preds = %for.body4
  %inc7 = add nsw i32 %i1.04, 1
  %cmp3 = icmp slt i32 %inc7, 10
  br i1 %cmp3, label %for.body4, label %for.end8

for.end8:                                         ; preds = %for.inc6
  %sum2.0.lcssa = phi i32 [ %add5, %for.inc6 ]
  %0 = add i32 %sum1.0.lcssa, %sum2.0.lcssa
  ret i32 %0
}
